faqs.question: Are admissions at Manav Bharti University - MBU entrance-based or merit-based?
faqs.answer: Manav Bharti University (MBU) adopts a merit-based, transparent, and cross-comparison admission method for its various programs, including B.Tech. Candidates will be admitted solely through an Entrance Test. The university considers the test score of all national or state-level entrance tests, such as JEE Mains, and if a student has not appeared in any national or state-level test, they have to take the University Entrance Exam. The minimum eligibility requirement for B.Tech is 12th PCM (45% Gen. & 40% SC/ST)

review_content.Placements: Actually I am a law student and there is no such placements in our course but in other course some students get placed but the package are very less and no big companies visits our campus for placements. Only around 30-40% of students gets placements from our university.
review_content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi speed is good but they don't provide that much amount of data to use. Library is huge and have lot of space and with all computer facilities in it. Mess food is not that good and also they don't have a big ground for sports activities which is kind of bad.

reviews_list.review_title: Facilities are average in our college.

review_content.Placements: From our B.Com course, about 100 students got placed from our college. The highest salary offered to us was Rs. 8,000 per month, while the lowest salary offered was Rs. 2,000 per month. Topmost companies visited our college after our final exams to hire us. About 60% of the students got jobs in those companies.
review_content.Infrastructure: Facilities provided by our college were above average, but the campus was good. Our college didn't provide Wi-Fi facility, but classrooms were huge. The hostel was very small, but the food provided in the mess was very good and hygienic. Our college provides excellent medical facilities for students.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering - Batch of 2016
reviews_list.review_title: Satisfactory university in terms of teaching as well as placement.

review_content.Placements: The university offers average placement and average package neither too high nor too low. The maximum number of placements were from CS and IT branch. Internship opportunities were also numerous. GET graduate engineer trainee was the role offered by most of the companies.
review_content.Infrastructure: The university is situated at a place where it is surrounded by scenic beauty all around. It has a huge campus and it emphasised on sports and games. Despite being in a hilly area the quality of food was good. Hostel was equipped with facilities like Wi-Fi, medical facilities.

reviews_list.review_title: Manav Bharti University is an abode in the snow with serene beauty and knowledgeable students.

review_content.Placements: There are hardly any placements until we graduated from college. Our professors used to tell us that it's better to learn the concepts before even applying them. It means that before even going to the corporate world, students must get good knowledge which is the arsenal for the battlefield of industry. The learnings can develop a new set of skillsets if one has an ample amount of knowledge.
review_content.Infrastructure: It's one of those few undergraduate colleges which has its distinct set of identity as far as its infrastructure is concerned. It is located at the peak of Himalayan trenches. This shining gorgeous campus is one of the best campuses you can find in the country. Starting from the campus which includes great Wi-Fi services, labs, smart classrooms, ample space in the library to a great plethora of sports and games facilities, everything is serene in that white snow of Solan district of Himachal Pradesh. One can definitely feel proud if they are studying on this campus.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering - Batch of 2020
reviews_list.review_title: The fee structure of our college is low, and the facilities provided are less.

review_content.Placements: Placement opportunities provided in the college are not good. In 2017, only 20 students got job opportunities through campus placement drives. I don't think that many companies visit the university during our placement season, so we are very much worried about our placements. The university needs to improve the placement facilities and should invite more companies to the campus.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of our college is well-organised. There's no place to play because the people who visit the university park their vehicles in the playground. There are no garden and transportation facilities. There is no shed at the bus stop, and the management is not serious about the future of the university.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering - Batch of 2021

review_content.Placements: Not too much percentage to have placement from the University because few numbers of companies visit the University. Last year in 2016 only 15-20 students get placement but not with the high payment. So I don't think that placements are good enough here.
review_content.Infrastructure: Classroom and library is good but there is nothing more than this. The Wi-Fi are placed everywhere but not working properly labs are good but some instruments are not in working condition. Sports rooms are average, every year a cricket tournament man was organised by the University and we take part very happily in the tournament.
